Backup Recommender Methodology
Affiliate disclosure
Some links on this site may be affiliate links. If you buy through them, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Recommendations are based on documented features, public information and stated use cases, not on payment alone. Sponsored placements are clearly marked.

Backup · ransomware resilience · small business

Meilleur logiciel de sauvegarde pour PME : cadre de décision pratique

Guide pragmatique pour choisir un logiciel de sauvegarde pour PME, en tenant compte de la résilience ransomware, des objectifs de reprise et du risque opérationnel.

Cold recommendation

Choose backup software by recovery reliability first, interface second, and price third. A cheap backup that cannot restore fast after ransomware is not a backup strategy.

Decision matrix

CriterionWhy it mattersMinimum acceptable signal
Immutable backupsLimits ransomware damage.Vendor documents immutability, object lock, or tamper-resistant retention.
Recovery testingBackups are worthless if restores fail.Clear restore workflow and test schedule.
RTO/RPO fitMatches tolerated downtime and data loss.Business can define target recovery time and recovery point.
MFA and role controlBackup consoles are high-value targets.MFA available for administrators.
Device and SaaS coverageSmall firms often mix laptops, cloud drives and Microsoft/Google accounts.Covers the systems actually used.

Starter shortlist categories

This first prototype does not rank vendors yet. Vendor pages will only be published after public evidence is collected for features, pricing model and affiliate status.

  • Cloud backup for endpoints
  • Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace backup
  • NAS and local image backup
  • Managed backup through MSP/MSSP

Next step

Use the recommender to identify the minimum stack to evaluate before comparing vendors.
